Phase-49.NAME: BIMI Sender Branding Asset Deployment
Phase-49.purpose: Deploy the prepared KrotPN BIMI SVG as a public HTTPS asset and document the DNS record needed for mailbox-provider sender-logo discovery.

scope.in: BIMI SVG normalization, frontend static asset placement, /.well-known/bimi nginx allowance, smoke verification, DNS runbook, and MyGRACE metadata.
scope.out: Resend API payload changes, email verification/reset token logic, install.sh, deploy scripts, docker-compose, TLS certificate issuance, VMC/CMC procurement, and guarantees that every mailbox provider displays the avatar.

Phase-49.problem: KrotPN has a prepared BIMI logo at /home/truffle/Загрузки/krotpn.svg, but it must be served from a stable HTTPS URL and referenced by DNS before non-Gmail/non-Apple providers can attempt sender-logo rendering.

steps.step-1: Confirm the prepared SVG is suitable for BIMI scope and record that sender avatars are provider-side, not a Resend send-API feature.
steps.step-2: Normalize the SVG root for BIMI Tiny P/S with version="1.2", baseProfile="tiny-ps", no root x/y attributes, title, desc, and size under 32 kilobytes.
steps.step-3: Place the normalized asset at frontend/public/.well-known/bimi/krotpn.svg so the frontend build copies it to the production static root.
steps.step-4: Update frontend/nginx.conf with a specific /.well-known/bimi/ static location before the hidden-file deny rule and preserve existing SPA/API behavior.
steps.step-5: Add scripts/phase49-bimi-branding-smoke.mjs to assert SVG compliance, route allowance, no unsafe SVG constructs, and no protected deploy/install changes.
steps.step-6: Run frontend npm build and verify the BIMI asset exists in dist/.well-known/bimi/krotpn.svg with unchanged content semantics.
steps.step-7: Run XML parse, MyGRACE lint, git diff --check, and protected deploy/install/docker-compose/TLS diff guard.
steps.step-8: After release deployment, operator validates live HTTPS headers and then publishes the default._bimi DNS TXT record.

data-flow.flow-1: Operator-provided SVG -> normalized BIMI Tiny P/S asset -> frontend public directory -> frontend container static root -> nginx/SNI HTTPS fallback -> https://krotpn.xyz/.well-known/bimi/krotpn.svg.
data-flow.flow-2: DNS default._bimi.krotpn.xyz TXT -> mailbox provider BIMI lookup -> provider fetches HTTPS SVG -> provider decides whether to render sender avatar.
data-flow.flow-3: Future VMC/CMC path can add an a=https://.../krotpn.pem tag without changing email delivery logic.

mental-walkthroughs.walkthrough-1: A non-Gmail mailbox provider receives a KrotPN email, verifies SPF/DKIM/DMARC, looks up default._bimi.krotpn.xyz, fetches the SVG URL, validates it, and may display the KrotPN avatar.
mental-walkthroughs.walkthrough-2: Gmail or Apple receives the same email but may not display the logo without VMC/CMC even though the BIMI asset and DNS record are valid.
mental-walkthroughs.walkthrough-3: A direct browser or curl request to /.well-known/bimi/krotpn.svg returns the SVG with image/svg+xml instead of the React app shell or a 403 hidden-file denial.

phase-gates.gate-1: SVG size is under 32 kilobytes and root includes version="1.2" plus baseProfile="tiny-ps".
phase-gates.gate-2: SVG has title and desc, no script, no animation, no external href/xlink references, no raster image, no base64, no foreignObject, and no root x/y attributes.
phase-gates.gate-3: frontend/nginx.conf permits /.well-known/bimi/ before the generic hidden-file deny rule and still denies unrelated hidden paths.
phase-gates.gate-4: Frontend production build includes the asset at dist/.well-known/bimi/krotpn.svg.
phase-gates.gate-5: install.sh, deploy scripts, docker-compose, TLS material, Resend provider code, and email token logic remain unchanged.
phase-gates.gate-6: Operator DNS instruction is explicit, uses default._bimi, and omits a= until VMC/CMC exists.

risks.risk-1: Mailbox providers can ignore valid BIMI records, especially without VMC/CMC; Phase-49 documents this limitation instead of treating rendering as guaranteed.
risks.risk-2: The frontend hidden-file deny rule currently blocks dot-prefixed paths unless /.well-known/bimi/ is allowed first.
risks.risk-3: SVG validators can differ, so Phase-49 checks the core BIMI constraints locally and leaves external BIMI validator proof as optional operator evidence.

matrix.module-local: Phase-49 smoke validates SVG compliance and nginx route ordering.
matrix.wave-level: Frontend build validates the public asset is copied into dist and the application still builds.
matrix.phase-level: XML parse, MyGRACE lint, git diff --check, and protected deploy/install diff guard prove metadata consistency and operational isolation.
matrix.live-optional: curl -I https://krotpn.xyz/.well-known/bimi/krotpn.svg verifies 200, image/svg+xml, and non-HTML response after clean deploy.

commands.command: python3 -m xml.etree.ElementTree docs/plans/Phase-49.xml docs/modules/M-067.xml docs/verification/V-M-067.xml docs/graph-index.xml docs/plan-index.xml docs/verification-index.xml
git diff --name-only HEAD -- install.sh docker-compose.yml nginx deploy deploy/mtproto-de-compose.yml .env.example

dns-instruction.note: If the DNS provider requires a fully qualified name, use default._bimi.krotpn.xyz. Do not add a= until a VMC/CMC PEM certificate is available.
